BEAVERHEAD-DEERLODGE NATIONAL FOREST SERVICE PERSONNEL ESTIMATED BETWEEN 200 TO 250 ACRES OF TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN FROM A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM THAT MOVED OVER THE AREA ON SUNDAY, AUGUST 11TH. THE BLOWN DOWN RESULTED IN A TRAIL LEADING BACK TO GRANITE LAKE IN THE TOBACCO ROOT MOUNTAINS TO BECOME BLOCKED. AT LEAST 4 PEOPLE BECAME STRANDED IN THE BACKCOUNTRY DUE TO THE BLOW DOWN OF TREES, WHICH RESULTED IN SEARCH AND RESCUE ATTEMPTS FROM THE MADISON COUNTY S&R, NATIONAL FOREST SERVICE FIRE TEAM, AND 2 AIR FORCE HELICOPTERS. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED UPON RADAR DATA AND LOCATION OF THE REPORT.
